name: core.openwop.mcp
description: Spec-canonical MCP (Model Context Protocol) pack. v1.0 four-node client baseline (list-tools, invoke-tool, read-resource, server-status) plus the v1.1 additions: full client-side surface (resources, prompts, completion, logging, ping) AND server-side (workflow IS an MCP server — server-trigger + expose-tool/resource/prompt + handle-sampling/elicitation + provide-roots). Tracks modelcontextprotocol.io 2025-06-18 spec via spec/v1/mcp-integration.md.
author: OpenWOP Working Group

nodes.label: MCP List Tools
nodes.description: Lists tools exposed by a registered MCP server. Useful for dynamic tool-discovery workflows and for diagnostics. Side-effectful: server-side tool catalog can change between calls. Cached via Layer-2 invocation log.

nodes.label: MCP Invoke Tool
nodes.description: Invokes a tool on a registered MCP server with caller-supplied arguments. Unlike LLM-mediated tool calls (which happen inside an AI node implicitly per spec/v1/mcp-integration.md), this node lets workflow authors invoke tools deterministically from the DAG. Side-effectful + cacheable.

nodes.label: MCP Read Resource
nodes.description: Reads a resource by URI from a registered MCP server. Returns the content + MIME type. Side-effectful (resource content may change between calls) + cacheable.

nodes.label: MCP Server Status
nodes.description: Health/availability check against a registered MCP server. Returns availability + reported name/version + measured latency. Side-effectful (real network round-trip).

nodes.label: MCP List Resources
nodes.description: JSON-RPC `resources/list` on a registered MCP server. Returns the static-resource catalog.

nodes.label: MCP List Resource Templates
nodes.description: JSON-RPC `resources/templates/list`. Returns templated resources (RFC 6570 URI templates).

nodes.label: MCP Subscribe Resource
nodes.description: JSON-RPC `resources/subscribe`. Trigger-style — fires downstream on `notifications/resources/updated`. Pair with an unsubscribe in the cleanup phase.

nodes.label: MCP List Prompts
nodes.description: JSON-RPC `prompts/list`. Lets workflows enumerate server-defined prompts before retrieving one.

nodes.label: MCP Get Prompt
nodes.description: JSON-RPC `prompts/get`. Fetches a server-defined prompt with caller-supplied arguments; output feeds an LLM node.

nodes.label: MCP Completion
nodes.description: JSON-RPC `completion/complete`. Server-side argument autocomplete for prompts / resource templates. Powers smart-input UI on other workflow nodes.

nodes.label: MCP Set Log Level
nodes.description: JSON-RPC `logging/setLevel`. Adjusts the server's emission level for the duration of the session.

nodes.label: MCP Log Listener
nodes.description: Subscribes to `notifications/message` events from the server. Streaming — emits one downstream event per log line.

nodes.label: MCP Ping
nodes.description: JSON-RPC `ping`. Latency probe; returns roundtripMs.

nodes.label: MCP Server Trigger
nodes.description: Workflow-as-MCP-server entry point. Mounts an MCP endpoint (stdio or streamable HTTP) and routes inbound `tools/call` / `resources/read` / `prompts/get` into the workflow.

nodes.label: MCP Expose Tool
nodes.description: Declarative: marks a workflow as an MCP tool with name + JSON-Schema input + output content shape. Consumed by the server-trigger when mounting.

nodes.label: MCP Expose Resource
nodes.description: Declarative: binds a workflow to a `resource_uri`. Emits `notifications/resources/updated` when the bound state changes.

nodes.label: MCP Expose Resource Template
nodes.description: Declarative: binds a workflow to an RFC 6570 URI template.

nodes.label: MCP Expose Prompt
nodes.description: Declarative: exposes a templated prompt with a variable list. Returns a GetPromptResult to inbound `prompts/get`.

nodes.label: MCP Handle Sampling
nodes.description: Handles inbound `sampling/createMessage` from a connected server. Routes to a workflow-chosen LLM (preserves consent — user's model under user's BYOK).

nodes.label: MCP Handle Elicitation
nodes.description: Handles inbound `elicitation/create` by suspending on a typed form (flat schema: string/number/boolean/enum, formats email/uri/date/date-time). Returns accept/decline/cancel + payload.

nodes.label: MCP Provide Roots
nodes.description: Answers `roots/list` from a workflow-managed list of `file://` URIs. Pairs with `host.fs` for actually serving content.
